text: I have posted this video in hopes that it will be useful for residents and surgeons learning the transabdominal preperitoneal inguinal hernia repair. The inguinal anatomy is complicated. Admittedly, I found it tricky to fully understand it when performing open inguinal hernia repairs in residency. The preperitoneal approach can be daunting at first, but I feel it is ultimately easier to understand from an internal perspective.
I have posted this video at full length and at real speed. A few points are worth mentioning. I do not use an assist port for this operation.

However, one is present here because I later performed a parasophageal hernia repair after the inguinal hernia repair was complete. All five ports were placed before the robot was docked. Therefore, the sutures and mesh were passed through one of the other available eight millimeter ports.

The surgeon uses a 10x15 cm ProGrip mesh to reinforce the repair. The peritoneal lining is closed with an absorbable 2-0 V-Lock suture, and a small defect in the peritoneum is closed with a 3-0 Vicryl suture on an S-H needle.

As long-winded as I am in other videos, I do not think I can talk for an entire twenty-two minutes, so I'll spare you the narration and simply entertain you with a few of my favorite songs. I hope you find this video useful. Thank you for watching.
